chasebank 2026/04/02 23:46

Ya but all the financial logic in the world doesn’t account for Elon god, number go up.
在 HN 中查看
strangattractor 2026/04/03 03:13
绝对是模因臭味领域。在我看来，埃隆很适合在目前不存在的没有竞争的事物上下大赌注。发展一家公司和应对竞争中的所有曲折的日常工作并没有那么多。
Definitely meme stonk territory. IMO Elon is good for making the big bets on things with no competition that don't currently exist. The day to day grind of growing a company and maneuvering all the twist and turns of competition not so much.
在 HN 中查看
slowmovintarget 2026/04/03 09:17
我和我的妻子谈论过这个（MBA、经济学硕士......）。她说，把这种公司当作老牌公司来对待（市盈率作为指标……等）是行不通的，因为公司还没有达到稳定状态。特斯拉不是，当它首次公开募股时，SpaceX 也不是。他们正在进行大量的研发工作，他们的收入不断被投入到新兴阶段。

我看到的对这两家公司的大多数嘲笑都是针对老牌公司的标准投资分析，或者是基于意识形态的简单仇恨。特斯拉和 SpaceX 就像乔布斯和沃兹在车库里的苹果一样。

轨道数据中心、月球和小行星采矿、比地球上任何其他商业实体高两个数量级的发射次数……SpaceX 正在变得更大。是的，他们可能会破产，但把他们当作微软、通用电气或三星来评估还没有多大意义。

I talked with my wife about this (MBA, Masters in Economics...). She said treating this kind of company like an established company (PE ratio as indicator... etc.) doesn't work because the company hasn't reached its steady state yet. Not Tesla, and, when it IPOs, not SpaceX either. They're in heavy R&D, where their revenues keep getting plowed into pushing through their emerging phase.

Most of the derision I see for both of these companies takes the form of standard investment analysis for established firms, or simple hatred on ideological grounds. Tesla and SpaceX are like Apple when it was Jobs & Woz in their garage.

Orbital data centers, moon and asteroid mining, more launches, by two orders of magnitude, than any other commercial entity on the planet... SpaceX is going bigger. Yes they might go bust, but evaluating them as though they were Microsoft, GE, or Samsung just doesn't make a lot of sense yet.

在 HN 中查看
bdangubic 2026/04/03 09:25
特斯拉？？？？！是一家已经营业十几年的公司吗？特斯拉就像苹果在车库里一样？？这太疯狂了，埃隆是上帝让散户投资者大军买入这种无稽之谈……我们有 12 年的机器人出租车梦想，他表现出无法将汽车从 A 点安全地移动到 B 点，现在他失败了，他要从沃兹的车库开采月球？天啊！太有趣了，读这样的东西并不是悲剧
Tesla????! The company that has been in business for more than a decade? Tesla is like Apple when it was in a garage?? That is f’ing wild, Elon is God to get an Army of retail investors buying into this kind of nonsense… We had 12 years of robotaxis dreams where he has shown incapable of moving a car safely from point A to point B and now that he failed at that he’s gonna mine the Moon from Woz’s garage? Geez! Too funny it it wasn’t tragic to read stuff like this

outside2344 2026/04/02 23:30
如果标准普尔改变规则，我就会卖掉我的指数基金，管他的税。

可悲的是，这并不是我们退休投资者面临的唯一垃圾。 OpenAI 也在伺机而动。

我确信我不是唯一一个。 这似乎对市场不利。

If S&P change their rules, I am going to sell my index funds, taxes be damned.

Sadly, this is not the only trash that is going to be hoisted on us retirement investors. OpenAI is waiting in the wings as well.

I am sure I am not the only one. That doesn't seem like it will be good for the market.

dweez 2026/04/03 00:43

If it's any consolation , VTI is free-float weighted so won't pick up very much SpaceX initially.

xhrpost 2026/04/03 03:03
VTI 是按市值自由流通量加权的，VOO 和 SPY 也是如此。那么，如果不是这些 ETF，那么本次 IPO 会出现哪些问题呢？
VTI is market cap free-float weighted, as is VOO and SPY. So what ETFs would this IPO be an issue with if not these?
在 HN 中查看
silotis 2026/04/03 05:23
到目前为止，只有 Nasdaq-100 认同 SpaceX 的特殊权重诡计。追踪纳斯达克 100 指数的最大基金是 QQQ。我只想说，如果你在QQ上有钱，你应该重新考虑这个职位。
So far only the Nasdaq-100 has gone along with SpaceX's special weighting chicanery. The biggest fund which tracks the Nasdaq-100 is QQQ. Suffice to say, if you have money in QQQ you should be re-considering that position.

SilverElfin 2026/04/03 00:17
之前有过关于被动投资者被欺骗的广泛讨论：

https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47392550

Michael Burry 也就此写了一篇长文：

https://x.com/michaeljburry/status/2032483200404992209

问题是我们能做些什么呢？纳斯达克已经最终确定了这些规则变更。看起来这件事已经被强行通过了，现在它正在发生。我不指望特朗普腐败的美国证券交易委员会对此采取任何行动。我们还能向谁求助？

There were extensive discussions previously about passive investors being taken for a ride:

https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47392550

And Michael Burry also wrote a long post about it:

https://x.com/michaeljburry/status/2032483200404992209

The question is what can we do about it? Nasdaq finalized these rule changes already. It seems like this got rammed through and now it is happening. And I don't expect Trump's corrupt SEC to do anything about it. Who else can we appeal to?

kortilla 2026/04/02 23:40

I used to agree until the xAI acquisition. Now IPO buyers will be buying debt from a shit tier dumpster fire to get access to SpaceX.
在 HN 中查看
davedx 2026/04/02 23:37
> 两阶段贴现现金流 (DCF) 模型。这是对 SpaceX 这样的高增长公司进行估值的黄金标准。

我不知道 DCF 是否是评估高增长公司的“黄金标准”。 IME 实际上很糟糕——并不是说真的有 好的 评估它们的方法； DCF 对于以下公司来说要好得多 不是 高增长。

高增长公司——尤其是马斯克经营的公司——本质上很难估值，原因如下：

- 他们有时 - 不可预测地 - 产生新的类别（想想星链）

- 变量太多，无法可靠地预测未来现金流量（例如，一家石油公司，其未来现金流量在很大程度上取决于油价，也可以通过以下方式进行预测） 一些 确定程度）

- 风险对高增长公司的影响要大得多，DCF 如何量化这一点？当然，您可以像 TFA 建议的那样提高无风险利率，但这只是一个粗略的衡量标准。考虑以下风险：特斯拉，您如何量化它们及其对未来现金流的影响？

> Two-Stage Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model. It is the gold standard for valuing a high-growth company like SpaceX.

I don't know if it's true that DCF is the "gold standard" for valuing high growth companies. IME it's actually quite bad -- not that there's really good ways to value them; more that DCF is much better for companies that aren't high growth.

High growth companies - especially ones run by Musk -- are intrinsically very hard to value, for reasons like:

- They sometimes - unpredictably - spawn new categories (think Starlink)

- There are too many variables to be able to reliably predict future cash flows (compared to say, an oil company, where future cash flows are largely dependent on oil prices, which can also be forecast with some degree of certainty)

- Risk has a much higher impact on a high growth company, how does DCF try to quantify that? Sure, you can ramp up your risk free rate like TFA suggests, but that's about as coarse a measure as it gets. Consider the risks to e.g. Tesla, how do you quantify them and their impact on its future cash flows?

在 HN 中查看
lokar 2026/04/03 00:59
但即使对于像星链这样的东西，我们也没有得到足够的细节来了解它是否会成为一项好生意，而且我怀疑我们会。
But even for something like starlink we have not been given enough details to understand if it would ever be a good business, and I doubt we will.
在 HN 中查看
eekjj 2026/04/03 00:17
实际上，所有这些都可以在贴现现金流中体现，贴现现金流将持续风险与持续经营的假设结合起来。

它没有包含失败风险，必须单独引入。

通过相对估值进行定价隐含 DCF……所以你实际上无法逃避它。如果你想在天上做点馅饼并凭空拉出一些数字 - 那就去做吧。

Actually all this can be captured in DCF, which incorporates continuous risks with the assumption of being a going concern.

What it does not incorporate is failure risk, which has to be brought in separately.

Pricing via relative valuation is implicitly DCF… so you can’t escape it actually. If you want to do some pie in the sky shit and pull a number out of thin air - go ahead.

在 HN 中查看
mattkrick 2026/04/02 23:31
这篇文章没有提及 SpaceX 不仅仅是一家火箭公司。与它捆绑在一起的是 xAI，它可能正在大幅亏损。将足够的风险打包在一起，以更高的价格出售给零售消费者。我们看过这部剧……
The post fails to mention that spaceX is not just a rocket company. Bundled with it is xAI, which is presumably losing money hand over fist. Package enough risk together and sell for a higher price to retail consumers. We’ve seen this play…

jmye 2026/04/03 00:43
> 不幸的是，做空它意味着打赌太空数据中心不会出现（我很乐意接受这个赌注）

这似乎是一个很容易获胜的赌注，尽管我不知道现在它被标记后你会做出什么反应。很难想象“太空数据中心”会成为在该网站上发帖的任何人的一生中有意义的产品或基础设施。

> Unfortunately, shorting it means betting that space data centers won’t happen (I’d happily take that bet)

This seems like an easy bet to win, though I don't know what you were responding to now that it's flagged. It's hard to imagine 'space data centers' being a meaningful product or infra in the collective lifetimes of anyone posting on this site.

steveBK123 2026/04/03 00:20
请记住，当通用人工智能到来时，金钱将变得毫无意义，因为我们只会拥有丰富的财富而没有工作。

但马斯克也需要获得 1T 美元的报酬，他还需要指数来改变规则，以便将更多的资金投入到他的超级 IPO 中。

这里没什么可看的。

Remember when AGI comes, money will be meaningless since we'll all just have abundance and no jobs.

But also Musk needs to get paid $1T, and he also needs indices to change their rules to pump more of your money into his giga-IPO.

Nothing to see here.

tristanj 2026/04/02 23:19

It would help if the author actually created a DCF model and shared it. I have seen others create their own which value SpaceX between $250 to $400 billion.
在 HN 中查看
neural_thing 2026/04/03 00:27
我设计了 IPO 前的赌注：

做多 RONB（持有大量 spacex），做空 ARKK（类似的成分，不含 SpaceX）——或者如果你有很多时间，你可以做空非 spacex RONB 持有的股票。计划在首次公开募股后立即出售

I've structured a pre IPO bet:

Long RONB (holds a ton of spacex), short ARKK (similar composition sans SpaceX) - or if you have a lot of time, you can short non-spacex RONB holdings. Planning to sell just after the IPO

在 HN 中查看
timzaman 2026/04/03 00:45
我对这里的一些黑客新闻人群感到非常失望。它曾经充满了有着有趣观点的细致入微的聪明人。现在看来，它是非常片面的——让我非常难过。人们似乎完全忘记了 SpaceX 是目前最“鼓舞人心”的公司之一。他们生产的东西（星际飞船）让成年人光是看着就会产生久违的鸡皮疙瘩和兴奋。 SpaceX 的技术和发射统计数据不言自明。我知道扩展指数很难，但尝试一下。这里的所有评论都像是在 2017 年针对特斯拉发表的，看看结果如何。
I have become so disillusioned by some of the Hacker News crowd on here. It used to be full of nuanced smart people with interesting takes. Nowadays it seems very one sided - makes me very sad. People seem to be completely forgetting that SpaceX is one of the most "inspiring" companies that currently exist. They produce things (Starship) that give adults long-lost goosebumps and excitement just looking at it. SpaceX tech and launch stats speak for themselves. I know its hard to extend an exponential, but just try. All the comments on here feel like they were made against Tesla in 2017, and look how that worked out.
在 HN 中查看
Sl1mb0 2026/04/03 01:18
> 我对这里的一些黑客新闻人群感到非常失望。

我也有同样的感觉，但这是由于像你这样的评论，似乎以创新的名义为任何事情辩解。人们感到起鸡皮疙瘩的只是他们根据万金油推销员兜售的内容来预测他们认为的未来可能是什么样子。我们又距离“全自动驾驶仅两年”有多久了？

> I have become so disillusioned by some of the Hacker News crowd on here.

I feel the exact same way, but it's due to comments like yours that seem to excuse anything in the name of innovation. The goosebumps people feel are them just projecting what they think the future may look like based on what the snake-oil salesmen are peddling. How long have we been "just 2 years away from fully-automated self-driving" again?

chasd00 2026/04/03 01:28
与SpaceX的不同之处在于你可以观看火箭飞行。您还可以观看他们实际构建的过程。鸡皮疙瘩就是从这里来的

一个实时摄像头视图，有很多 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mhJRzQsLZGg

这家伙大约每周发布一次视频 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=X_YDnrjf3G8&t=241s

这个航空摄影频道也每周左右都会记录博卡奇卡。 SpaceX 绝对是从各个角度都受到关注的。 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tnPcEFla3Dw

The difference with spacex is you can watch the rockets fly. You can also watch them actually build. That's where the goosebumps come from

one livecam view, there are many https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mhJRzQsLZGg

this guy releases videos about once a week or so https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=X_YDnrjf3G8&t=241s

this aerial photography channel documents bocachica every week or so too. Spacex is absolutely watched from every angle. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tnPcEFla3Dw

这与它是否值得估值无关

chasd00 2026/04/03 03:42
我的意思是散户投资者并不关心估值，他们会根据他们看到的情况进行交易。因此，由于散户投资者的原因，预计价格会出现大幅波动。
what i'm saying is retail investors don't care about the valuation and will trade on what they see. So expect a lot of ups and down in the price because of retail investors.
在 HN 中查看
margalabargala 2026/04/03 01:24
难道“制造非常酷的可重复使用火箭的公司也可能从事证券欺诈”之类的事情不正是您正在寻找的细微差别吗？

“SpaceX 很鼓舞人心！善待他们！”

“SpaceX 既不全是好，也不全是坏！他们会做好事，也会做坏事。”

其中一个有细微差别，另一个则没有。

Isn't something like "the company that makes really cool reusable rockets may also be engaging in securities fraud" exactly the sort of nuance you're looking for?

"SpaceX is inspiring! Be nice to them!"

"SpaceX is neither all good nor all bad! They do good things and bad things."

One of those has nuance, the other does not.

AndrewKemendo 2026/04/03 00:57
世界终于醒悟到“殖民火星”是一派胡言。

就像“全自动驾驶”过去（现在仍然）都是假胡言乱语，用来做富人总是做的事情：

从你那里获取（通过税收和通货膨胀支出）并通过政府合同给予自己，这些政府合同是通过游说者和监管捕获完成的。

马斯克实际上通过 DOGE 突袭了政府，以确保这些合同得以完成。他的全部伎俩就是通过补贴或直接合同来获得政府的好处

你真的在表达这一点吗？

The world finally woke up to the fact that “colonizing mars” was fake bullshit.

The same way “full self driving” was (and is) fake bullshit used to do what the rich always do:

Take from you (via taxes and inflation spending) and give to themselves via government contracts that are done via lobbyists and regulatory capture.

Musk literally raided the govt via DOGE to ensure these contracts got done. His entire grift is to get on the government tit via subsidies or direct contracts

Are you really making this point?

nickburns 2026/04/03 01:03

chasd00 2026/04/03 01:21
对于散户投资者来说，每次失败的引擎测试、压力测试或其他任何事情都将成为卖出事件。每一次成功的发射、发动机交付、玻璃安装都将成为一次购买事件。我敢打赌，像 Nasaspaceflight 这样的 YouTube 博主以及其他通过每个网站的实时摄像头跟踪 Spacex 一举一动的人，都会将股价添加到他们的直播中。
for retail investors every failed engine test, pressure test, or anything else is going to be a sell event. Every succesful launch, engine delivery, installed pane of glass is going to be a buy event. The youtubers like Nasaspaceflight and the others who stalk Spacex's every move with livecams at every site are going to add the stock price to their streams i bet.

bob1029 2026/04/03 00:21
令人难以置信的强制需求的潜力让我考虑参与一段时间。我对轨道上的数据中心之类的东西没有信心，但我对其他人的贪婪和鲁莽有坚定的信心。
The potential for incredible forced demand has me considering participating for a period of time. I have no faith in things like datacenters in orbit, but I do have strong faith in the greed and recklessness of others.

paxys 2026/04/03 01:13
“我知道这是一个骗局，但我会在最高点卖出，然后在所有傻瓜之前退出”——过去十年中的每一位模因股票和加密货币泡沫投资者。最终，你将成为其他人（风险投资家、银行、内部人士）从中赚钱的傻瓜。
"I know it's a scam, but I'll sell at the peak and get out before all the suckers" - every meme stock and crypto bubble investor in the last decade. Ultimately you will be the sucker that others (VCs, banks, insiders) will make money off of.

kjkjadksj 2026/04/02 23:40
当我们唯一可行的发射能力公开交易并且只展望未来一个季度时，这将成为一场马戏表演。我猜这就是我们所知的火箭开发的终结。希望学术界能够拯救我们并继续进行基础研究。然而，白宫里的猩猩却不愿意资助任何科学研究。
Going to be a circus when our only viable launch capacity is publicly traded and only looking a quarter ahead. Guess that is the end of rocket development as we know it. Hopefully academia bails us out and carries on fundamental research. Then again the orangutan is in the whitehouse disinclined to fund any science.

在 HN 中查看
wat10000 2026/04/02 23:36
为了使这一估值有意义，您押注于以下一项或多项：

1. 轨道数据中心不仅成为现实，而且成为主导。

2. Grok 从一个主要用于没有护栏的二线模型变成了超越所有其他产品的一步。

3. Twitter实现了“一切应用”的野心，成为西方的微信。

4. Starship 不仅可以正常飞行，而且还找到了一个比 Falcon 9 获得更多数量级业务的利基市场。类似地对地客运的水平大大取代了航空公司。

所有这些看起来都极不可能。我相当看好 SpaceX，但它只是一个“正常”的企业。星舰展现出了希望。猎鹰 9 号是一款廉价的主力飞机。 Starlink 似乎只是印钞票。但价值不及一万亿美元。

For this valuation to make sense, you’re betting on one or more of:

1. Orbital data centers become not only a real thing, but a dominant thing.

2. Grok goes from being a second-tier model mostly useful for not having guardrails to being a step above all other offerings.

3. Twitter realizes its “everything app” ambitions and becomes the WeChat of the West.

4. Starship not only flies operationally, but finds a niche with orders of magnitude more business than Falcon 9 gets. Something like Earth-to-Earth passenger transport at a level that substantially displaces airlines.

All of which seem extremely unlikely. I’m fairly bullish on SpaceX, but as something of a “normal” business. Starship shows promise. Falcon 9 is a cheap workhorse. Starlink seems to just print money. But not anything like a trillion dollars’ worth.

在 HN 中查看
vessenes 2026/04/02 23:49
最合理的分析在这里！但你忘记了：“零售业喜欢它并购买它，并提供足够的资本来稳定。”

我也喜欢 SpaceX——这里的许多孩子似乎忘记了一件事，埃隆过去非常巧妙地管理着极其可怕的资本和盈利状况——十年前特斯拉的上述名单看起来要糟糕得多。

这对于你的名单上的成功并不是决定性的，但它确实意味着你可以像对待一个长期的电话一样对待公司：它几乎肯定不会消失。

Most reasonable analysis here! But you forgot: “retail loves it and buys it, providing capital sufficient to stabilize.”

I also like SpaceX - one thing many of the kids around here seem to forget is that elon has managed extremely dire capital and earnings situations very ably in the past - the above list for Tesla ten years ago looked much much worse.

This isn’t dispositive to success on your list but it does mean you can treat the company more like a long call : it almost certainly won’t go away.

在 HN 中查看
danny_codes 2026/04/03 00:48
他会再次得到美联储的救助吗？ SpaceX 第三次发射失败得到了国防部的救助。特斯拉得到了奥巴马的救助。

可能吧。我不知道被救助是否是商业头脑的标志。也许政治敏锐？无论如何，救助当今的特斯拉或 SpaceX 将非常昂贵，不清楚纳税人是否会为此付出代价。

这大概就是埃隆瞄准零售业作为救助者的原因

Will he get bailed out by the Feds again though? SpaceX launch 3 failure was bailed out by the DOD. Tesla was bailed out by Obama.

Possibly. I don’t know if being bailed out is a sign of business acumen. Maybe political acumen? In any case bailing out present day Tesla or SpaceX will be very expensive, unclear of taxpayers will go for it.

Which is presumably why Elon is aiming for retail as the bail-outers

在 HN 中查看
vessenes 2026/04/03 02:17
SpaceX 显然不需要救助。我不了解 Launch 3 的历史，所以我无法发表意见，但说特斯拉是被奥巴马救助的就太还原了。埃隆是美国历史上最大的单一纳税人。

这不是 Elon 想要的救助，也不是 SX 的“安全”。他希望资本尽快投入到太空建设中；他需要零售资本来做到这一点。

SpaceX is emphatically not in need of a bailout. I don't know the history of launch 3, so I can't opine, but saying Tesla was bailed out by Obama is reductive in the extreme. Elon is the single largest taxpayer in US history.

It's not a bail-out Elon wants, nor is it 'safety' for SX. He wants capital to go build out space as fast as possible; he needs retail capital to do it.

在 HN 中查看
wat10000 2026/04/03 02:54
人们（我敢肯定，是故意的）将救助与服务付款混为一谈。

另一位评论者可能指的是 NASA 猎鹰 9 号和龙飞船 CRS 合同，该合同是在第三次发射失败后授予的，几乎拯救了该公司。但这是为国际空间站提供补给的付款，就像他们对其他公司所做的那样，而不是救助。

2010 年，特斯拉确实在其早期历史的关键时刻获得了美国能源部的贷款。值得注意的是，这笔贷款金额约为 2008 年政府救助传统汽车制造商支出的 5%，约占马斯克当前净资产的 0.5%。

People mix up (deliberately, I'm pretty sure) bailouts with payments for services.

The other commenter might be referring to the NASA CRS contract for Falcon 9 and Dragon which was awarded after that third failed launch and pretty much saved the company. But that was payment for providing resupply to the ISS, just like they've done with other companies, not a bailout.

Tesla did get a DoE loan in 2010 at a critical point its early history. It's worth noting that the amount of this loan was approximately 5% what the government spent bailing out legacy automakers in 2008, and about 0.5% of Musk's current net worth.

在 HN 中查看
marcosdumay 2026/04/03 01:42
关于#1，我搜索的第一个结果是：

> 2025年全球数据中心市场规模预计为3838.2亿美元

获得整个市场并获得与苹果类似的盈利能力将使 SpaceX 成为一只被高估的股票，只有在有增长可能性的情况下才有意义。

第 2 点，取代世界上所有白领工作并获得他们 100% 的薪水将使 SpaceX 的市盈率接近 2。

在#3 上，Visa 的收入似乎占所有数据中心的 10% 左右。所以，不，即使对于高增长的企业来说，这还不够。

关于#4，IATA 表示航空旅行市场规模约为 $800B。因此，即使 SpaceX 获得全部收益，它的市盈率仍将大于 2。

所以，是的，他们要么创造一个全能的通用人工智能，要么创造一些比飞机更便宜的火箭……而且他们最好成为该市场上唯一的公司，并获得他们创造的大部分价值。

“极不可能”是一个非常轻描淡写的说法。

About #1, the first result in my search says:

> the global data center market size was estimated at USD 383.82 billion in 2025

Getting that entire market, with Apple-like profitability would leave SpaceX as an overvalued stock that only makes sense if there are possibilities for growth.

On #2, replacing every single white-collar job on the world and capturing 100% of their salary would leave SpaceX with a P/E close to 2.

On #3, Visa seems to have earnings of about 10% of all datacenters up there. So, no, that's not enough even for a high-growth business.

On #4, IATA says the air-travel market is about $800B large. So, if SpaceX gets all of it, it would still have a P/E larger than 2.

So yeah, either they create an all-capable AGI or they create some rocket that is cheaper to run than an airplane... And they better be the only ones on that market, and capture most of the value they create.

"Extremely unlikely" is a huge understatement.

在 HN 中查看
[deleted] 2026/04/03 00:13 deleted

[已删除]

[deleted]

在 HN 中查看
amluto 2026/04/03 00:10
> Starlink 似乎只是印钞票。

是吗？ 与历史上的通信卫星相比，这些卫星的价格非常便宜，但星链需要大量卫星，而且它们的贬值速度惊人。

与我个人最喜欢的通信介质单模光纤相比。 20-30 年前的 SMF 仍然有效，与大多数当代波长兼容，并且如果用户愿意在末端安装精美的光学器件和多路复用器，则每条链可以承载极高的带宽，或者可以以 20 年前几乎难以想象的低收发器价格承载较低的速度。

五年后，星链卫星的价值似乎为零，甚至略有负值。

> Starlink seems to just print money.

Does it? Those satellites are individually dirt cheap compared to historical communication satellites, but Starlink requires a whole lot of them and they depreciate outrageously quickly.

Compare to my personal favorite communication medium, single-mode-fiber. SMF from 20-30 years ago still works, is compatible with most current-generation wavelengths, and can carry extremely high bandwidth per strand if users are willing to put fancy optics and muxes at the ends or can carry lower speeds at transceiver prices that would have been almost unimaginably low 20 years ago.

Starlink satellites seem to have zero or even slightly negative value after five years.

在 HN 中查看
wat10000 2026/04/03 02:04
将光纤接入房屋相对昂贵，尤其是星链主要市场农村地区的房屋。星链卫星的成本要高得多，但可以为许多客户提供服务。

假设一颗 Starlink 卫星的总成本为 200 万美元。 （他们一次发射大约 25 颗卫星，发射成本约为 2500 万美元，再加上卫星本身和运营的 100 万美元。）目前他们在轨道上拥有大约 10,000 颗卫星，以及大约 1000 万客户。每颗卫星大约有 1,000 个客户，因此每个客户的五年成本为 2,000 美元。这比在农村房屋铺设光纤的成本要低得多。星链在其主要市场上几乎处于垄断地位（地面电信通常至少是双寡头垄断），因此他们可以收取更高的费用。我每月支付 85 美元购买对称千兆光纤。 Starlink 的 200Mbps 收费为 80 美元/月，“最大”速率为 120 美元/月。最重要的是，他们可以向客机和游轮等商业用户收取巨额费用。

根据 https://www.reuters.com/business/finance/spacex-generated-ab...星链去年的收入超过 80 亿美元。他们每年需要发射 2,000 颗卫星来维持当前的卫星群。如果 200 万美元是他们的准确价格标签，那么每年就是 40 亿美元。利润不错，还有很大的发展空间。

Getting fiber to a house is relatively expensive, especially houses in more rural areas which is Starlink's main market. A Starlink satellite costs a lot more but can serve many customers.

Let's say a Starlink satellite costs $2 million all-in. (They launch about 25 at a time, the launch costs something like $25 million, add in another million for the satellite itself and operations.) They have about 10,000 satellites in orbit currently, and about 10 million customers. That's about 1,000 customers per satellite, so a five-year cost of $2,000 per customer. That's a fair bit less than it costs to run fiber to a rural house. And Starlink is pretty much a monopoly in their main markets (terrestrial telecoms is usually at least a duopoly) so they can charge more. I pay $85/month for symmetric gigabit fiber. Starlink charges $80/month for 200Mbps, or $120/month for "max." On top of that, they can charge enormous amounts for commercial users like airliners and cruise ships.

According to https://www.reuters.com/business/finance/spacex-generated-ab..., Starlink revenue last year was north of $8 billion. They'd need to launch 2,000 satellites per year to maintain the current fleet. If $2 million is an accurate price tag for them, then that's $4 billion/year. Pretty nice profit, and there's a lot of room for growth.

在 HN 中查看
amluto 2026/04/03 03:40
这看起来大体上是正确的，但有一些事情需要注意。

一旦安装了光纤，无限期的维护并不是特别昂贵。 每个客户 2000 美元需要每五年再次支付，而对于光纤来说，这更接近于一次性成本。 （公平地说，光纤仍然会贬值并受到损坏。）并且光纤不会 那 安装成本昂贵：星链显然在真正的农村地区胜出，但对于低密度郊区来说，情况就不那么明显了。

与高质量的 DOCSIS 光纤部署相比，Starlink 的性能并不出色，因此他们将在后者提供良好服务的领域陷入困境，这些领域覆盖了相当多的有支付能力的人口，至少在发达市场如此。 因此，可解决的市场问题总量有限。

当然，星链可能还有其他有价值的应用，尤其是军事应用。

This seems generally correct, but there are some things to note.

Once fiber is installed, it’s not particularly expensive to maintain, indefinitely. That $2k/customer needs to be paid again every five years, whereas for fiber it’s much closer to being a one time cost. (To be fair, fiber still depreciates and gets damaged.) And fiber is not that expensive to install: Starlink clearly wins for truly rural areas, but for merely low-density suburban areas it’s not nearly so clear.

Starlink’s performance is not awesome compared to high quality DOCSIS fiber deployments, so they will struggle in areas that are well served by the latter, which covers quite a lot of the population by ability to pay, at least in developed markets. So there’s a limited total addressable market issue.

Of course, Starlink may have other valuable applications, especially military.

在 HN 中查看
wat10000 2026/04/03 06:04
我不认为他们在任何有中等水平的地面选择可用的地方都没有竞争力。但有足够多的地方无法提供这些服务，但足以为他们带来 1000 万客户并不断增长，这就足够了。

如果我是他们，我最担心的将是蜂窝连接的扩建。良好的 5G 连接可能具有竞争力。但如果他们的直接到蜂窝的东西成功了，我们可能会看到相反的情况：农村蜂窝基础设施停止建设甚至减少，因为通过卫星提供覆盖更便宜。

I don't think they're competitive anywhere a halfway decent terrestrial option is available. But there are enough places where those aren't available to get them 10 million customers and growing, which is enough.

If I were them, my big concern would be getting overtaken by the buildout of cellular connectivity. A good 5G connection could be competitive. But if their direct-to-cell stuff works out, we might see the opposite: rural cellular infrastructure stops being built out or even diminishes because it's cheaper to provide coverage by satellite.

在 HN 中查看
benj111 2026/04/02 23:52
这是一家埃隆公司，估值永远没有意义。
It's an Elon company, the valuation is never going to make sense.
在 HN 中查看
nutjob2 2026/04/02 23:03
> 马斯克是一个讲故事的人。

马斯克就是个胡说八道的人。

从任何客观标准来看都是如此。他超越了“营销”，只是通过撒谎来保持成功。他没有被追究责任，这是对美国证券交易委员会和整个美国公共股权体系的控诉。

> Musk is a storyteller.

Musk is a bullshitter.

This is true by any objective measure. He goes beyond "marketing" and just tells lies to keep the balls in the air. That he's not held to account is an indictment of the SEC and the whole public equity system in the US.

His real benefactors are not the public...https://ioc.exchange/@muskfiles/116333241408716236
在 HN 中查看
tartoran 2026/04/03 01:06
“SpaceX 缺乏真正的火星计划的硬件或具有科学可行性的计划。科学界早就认识到了这种假象。《自然》杂志上发表的一项可行性研究明确得出结论，使用星际飞船进行载人火星任务是行不通的。飞船巨大的干重造成了严重的 Delta-V 缺陷，使得返回飞行实际上不可能。此外，该架构缺乏闭环生命支持，依赖于大规模、不存在的核能和水利开采基础设施。星际飞船不是为火星建造的，而是一个具有低特征能量（C3）的重型运载工具，这只是将质量驱动到近地轨道星座的合理设计，该轨道完美地反映了五角大楼几十年前的目标，最近表现为金顶。

20 世纪 80 年代，迈克尔·D·格里芬 (Michael D. Griffin) 设计了“辉煌卵石”(Brilliant Pebbles)，这是一个由近地轨道上数千颗武器化卫星组成的全球导弹拦截网络。由于 DC-X 可重复使用火箭计划未能降低发射成本，该计划于 20 世纪 90 年代与里根的战略防御计划一起消亡。建筑梦想通过“新空间”的倡导得以延续。格里芬是火星协会的联合创始人，并在彼得·蒂尔引起他的注意后招募了埃隆·马斯克。 2001年，格里芬和年轻的马斯克一起前往俄罗斯考察洲际弹道导弹。 SpaceX 的构想是在返航途中解决导致 Brilliant Pebbles 遇难的发射瓶颈问题。马斯克后来承认，该公司只是“继续 DC-X 项目的伟大工作”，最终是格里芬（后来担任 NASA 局长）授予了数十亿美元的合同，使零经验的 SpaceX 免于破产。

当格温·肖特韦尔 (Gwynne Shotwell) 于 2018 年公开证实该公司愿意发射进攻性武器时，SpaceX 的掩码开始下滑。同年，格里芬回到五角大楼，成立了太空发展局，负责建立一个用于高超音速导弹跟踪的扩散 LEO 星座。 2019年，美国将军特伦斯·奥肖内西向参议院推销“神盾局”——一种分层轨道导弹防御系统。不久之后，奥肖内西从军队退役并加入 SpaceX，领导他们谨慎的新部门：Starshield。

三十年后，辉煌卵石终于以金顶的形式出现。据路透社报道，马斯克的 Starshield 是建造这款 SDI 继任者的领跑者，为五角大楼提供了包含数千颗武器卫星的金顶架构。星盾公司已经将这些军用卫星与标准星链卫星一起部署。

火星是招募人才、激发公众想象力和获得资本的必要神话。但正如《自然》研究所证明的那样，星舰在物理上从来不具备行星殖民的能力。 SpaceX 实际提供的能力……廉价的质量入轨和快速卫星补给……正是金顶的先决条件。 ”

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Golden_Dome_(missile_defense_s...

"SpaceX lacks the hardware or a plan with scientific viability for a genuine Mars program. The scientific community has long recognized the facade. A feasibility study published in the journal Nature definitively concluded that a crewed Mars mission using Starship is unworkable. The vehicle’s massive dry weight creates a severe Delta-v deficit, making a return flight physically impossible. Furthermore, the architecture lacks closed-loop life support and relies on massive, non-existent nuclear power and water-mining infrastructure. Instead of building for Mars, Starship is a heavy-lift vehicle with low characteristic energy (C3). This is only a reasonable design for driving mass to low-Earth-orbit constellations—a trajectory that perfectly mirrors decades-old Pentagon objectives, that recently has manifested as Golden Dome.

In the 1980s, Michael D. Griffin architected "Brilliant Pebbles," a global missile-interceptor network made up of thousands of weaponized satellites in Low Earth Orbit. It died alongside Reagan's Strategic Defense Initiative in 1990s after the DC-X reusable rocket program failed to lower launch costs. The architectural dream survived through "New Space" advocacy. Griffin co-founded the Mars Society and recruited Elon Musk after he was brought to his attention by Peter Thiel. In 2001 Griffin and the young Musk traveled together to Russia to examine ICBMs. SpaceX was conceived on the flight home to solve the exact launch bottleneck that killed Brilliant Pebbles. Musk later admitted the company was simply "continuing the great work of the DC-X project," and it was ultimately Griffin—later acting as NASA Administrator—who awarded billions of dollars in contracts that saved a zero-experience SpaceX from bankruptcy.

SpaceX masking began to slip when Gwynne Shotwell publicly confirmed the company's willingness to launch offensive weapons in 2018. That same year, Griffin returned to the Pentagon to establish the Space Development Agency, mandated to build a proliferated LEO constellation for hypersonic missile tracking. In 2019, U.S. General Terrence O'Shaughnessy pitched the Senate on "SHIELD"-a layered orbital missile defense system. Shortly after, O'Shaughnessy retired from the military and joined SpaceX to lead their discreet new division: Starshield.

Three decades later, Brilliant Pebbles is finally materializing as Golden Dome. As Reuters reported, Musk's Starshield is the frontrunner to build this classified SDI successor, pitching the Pentagon on a Golden Dome architecture involving thousands of weapon satellites. Starshield is already deploying these military satellites alongside standard Starlink satellites.

Mars was the necessary myth to recruit talent, capture public imagination, and secure capital. But as the Nature study proves, Starship was never physically capable of planetary colonization. The capabilities SpaceX actually delivered...cheap mass-to-orbit and rapid satellite replenishment...are the exact prerequisites of Golden Dome. "

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Golden_Dome_(missile_defense_s...

在 HN 中查看
guywithahat 2026/04/02 23:00
> SpaceX 于 2026 年进行 IPO 不太可能达到早期亚马逊或谷歌 500 倍或 1,000 倍的回报率

我认为这是不对的；当谷歌首次公开募股时，人们的情绪是他们拥有一个成功的产品：搜索，并且该股票预计将跟踪搜索。现在他们拥有一整套成功的产品。

同样，SoaceX 被视为一家火箭公司，但他们可能会继续扩大产品范围，而且据我们所知，他们未来的一些产品可能会更大、利润更高。

> The SpaceX IPO in 2026 is unlikely to mirror the 500x or 1,000x returns of early Amazon or Google

I don't think this is right; when Google first IPO'd the sentiment was that they had a single successful product, search, and the stock was expected to track search. Now they have a whole suite of successful products.

Similarily SoaceX is viewed as a rocket company, but they're likely to continue to expand their product range, and for all we know some of their future products could be bigger and more profitable.

在 HN 中查看
u1hcw9nx 2026/04/02 23:09
说一个数字。

您认为 SpaceX 的估值应该是多少才算定价过高？

如果 1.75T 就可以了。 $5T 太多了吗？我认为这个想法是，超过 1.5 的估值已经被考虑在内（正如叙述谬误）

Say a number.

In your opinion how much SpaceX should be valued to be overpriced?

If $1.75T is OK. Is $5T too much? I think the idea is that with over 1.5 valuation that is already taken into account (as is the narrative fallacy)

在 HN 中查看
guywithahat 2026/04/03 00:32
我不知道正确的价值是多少，但我对IPO的理解是银行首先购买（承销）股票，然后上市。这表明一家大型银行对该公司进行了大规模的财务押注，我相信他们了解市场和 SpaceX 目前的价值。

我知道这是一个蹩脚的答案，因为这是对权威的诉求，但我对股价没有任何看法，除了知识渊博的人同意这是公平的，并投入了大量自己的钱。

我的看法是，我认为他们有足够的空间来扩大市场和增长。我还知道 SpaceX 的 EBITDA 对于一家硬件公司来说高得离谱，这表明这是一个利润丰厚的行业，其他人很难以较低的经常性成本进入。在我看来，他们可能会继续以 150 亿美元的收入增长，而且这种增长很可能是有利可图的。

I don't know what the correct value is, however my understanding of IPO's is that a bank buys (underwrites) the shares first and then lists them. This would suggest a large bank has taken a massive financial bet on the company, and I trust they understand the market and SpaceX's current value.

I know this is a lame answer because it's an appeal to authority, but I don't have an opinion on the share price other than very knowledgeable people have agreed it's fair and put up a lot of their own money.

What I do have an opinion on is that I think there's plenty of room for them to expand the market and grow. I also know the EBITDA for SpaceX is outrageously high for a hardware company, would would suggest it's a lucrative industry that others have trouble entering with low recurring costs. It seems likely to me they could continue to grow on 15 billion of revenue, and this growth is likely to be profitable.

在 HN 中查看
lokar 2026/04/03 01:07
承销商几乎不承担任何风险，并且与他们所承担的风险相比，其上行空间要高得多。 你永远不应该将银行参与承销 IPO 视为对业务长期（甚至中期）前景的任何形式的认可。
The underwriters take almost no risk, and have a much higher upside compared to the risk they do take. You should never view the participation of a bank in underwriting an IPO as any kind of endorsement of the long (or even mid) term prospects for the business.
在 HN 中查看
sifar 2026/04/03 01:04
>> 我相信他们了解市场和 SpaceX 目前的价值

更有可能的是，当事人 知道 他们可以创造/操纵条件以使其成功。

>> I trust they understand the market and SpaceX's current value

It is more likely that the parties involved know that they can create/manipulate conditions to make it a success.

Seems ludicrous. They're starting at half of Googles current market cap and have to 1000x.

dan353hehe 2026/04/02 23:16
> 现在他们拥有一整套成功的产品。

比如什么？除了广告之外，他们还有什么真正能带来收入的吗？

SpaceX 也是 Twitter(X) 和 Xai。所以他们已经有几种正在赔钱的产品了。不知道除了太空中的人工智能数据中心之外，他们还在筹划什么。

> Now they have a whole suite of successful products.

Like what? Do they have anything that actually brings in income other than advertising?

SpaceX also is Twitter(X), and Xai. So they already have several products that are loosing them money. Not sure what else they have in the pipeline other then ai data centers in space.

foobarian 2026/04/02 23:05

在 HN 中查看
nutjob2 2026/04/02 23:06
当然可以，但这纯粹是猜测。这个价格表明的不是猜测，而是某种未具体说明的巨大成功的确定性。

随着谷歌更加成功并创造了新产品，他们的价格上涨了。他们并没有试图从投资者那里预先获取资金来购买蒸气。

Sure, but that's pure speculation. The price suggests not speculation but certainty about some unspecified and massive success.

Google's price went up as they were more successful and created new products. They didn't try to extract money upfront from investors for vapor.

在 HN 中查看
guywithahat 2026/04/03 00:20
我认为所有股票价格都是投机性的，他们可能会在一个月内将股价调整为四分之一，也可能是四倍。当我看到一家公司时，我发现自己总是感到惊讶，思考“他们还能做什么？”，然后看着这家公司的价值再次爆炸式增长。我不同意他们的股价假设了一些未具体说明的巨大成功
I would argue all stock prices are speculative, they could quarter their share price in a month or they could 4x it. I've found myself constantly surprised when I look at a company, think "what else could they do?", and then watch the company explode in value again. I disagree that their share price assumes some unspecified massive success
在 HN 中查看
